response.justification: Candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with broad full-stack and developer-tooling experience (test frameworks in D, CI/CD setup, MCP tooling), but the resume shows no explicit people-management or team-leadership tenure, which is the core of this role. Mobile-platform exposure is limited to a brief iOS/Apple TV stint nearly a decade ago. Without evidence of managing engineers, conducting one-on-ones, or leading organizational change, this is a mismatch for a Level II Manager position.
strengths: 15+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and backend
Genuine developer-tooling background: test runners (Trial), assertion libs (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD, MCP code generators
Strong community/teaching footprint — workshops, Selenium course, dojos — signals mentoring aptitude
concerns: No explicit people-management role or direct reports stated anywhere on the resume
Mobile-platform experience is thin and dated (WatchUp iOS/tvOS, 2015–2016)
No evidence of leading organizational change or owning team roadmaps at scale
